title: feature: Add --top-refs flag
body:
Allow to only synchronize top n refs for image
Use cases:
- Only sync the numbers of the latest refs at the given time when the synchronization happens
- If you have a bunch of images managed by your custom tooling and want to migrate to `skopeo sync`, this will help you to simplify the complicated of determining regex* for each image to not reach the upstream registries' pull limit
- If you have some garbage collectors in your registry, this will also help reduce the cost to exclude the tags which are supposed to be cleaned up with the skopeo images' configuration

P/S: I'm new to Go, so feel free to discard this PR if it totally implemented in the wrong way, but we would like to have this feature.

comments.body:
Thanks, @mtrmac for checking. I'm interested in only `sync` with src/dest's transport is`docker` only, also quickly tried to check the ordering of ref returned, and it looks already sorted by uploaded date, so maybe it is just a coincidence. In API spec https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/api/#tags also is not mentioning anything about the ordering requirement.

r/d `latest image` term, I meant for the last uploaded, but I think it will be the same as the largest version if the image follows some semantic versioning.

IMO if we are able to implement https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1756, then it can be easy to add this feature by sorting semantic versioning and just returning a subset of the largest version ones

We have added sorting by Semantic versioning and getting the top largest tags. Maybe it could help someone who has the same use cases.

@mtrmac Do you think this request makes sense? It somehow relates to this https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1756 as well, not sure if I should open this PR here, or move the discussion to that issue.

Of the two features, I think limiting to a minimum version is probably more useful — limiting to N recent releases makes it non-obvious what will _actually_ be synced, especially for users who are on an older stable stream (suppose 3.1.0 exists for two years, there are dozens of releases ≥3.2.0, and 3.1.1 was just released).

OTOH that minimum version would need to be manually updated.

Strictly speaking, nothing prevents us from having both… though there’s some value to simplicity.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@helobinvn are you still interested in this?
Hi @rhatdan, yes. We're still using this patch on our end
Could you rebase the PR then.
@rhatdan I have rebased, but still, 1 test is failing, I guess it is unrelated to this PR? Please help to review, thx you!
Did you modify the PR to match @mtrmac requests?
I think I did, but not sure if this change covers enough cases or not, @mtrmac could you help to do CR again, pls?
@helobinvn thanks for the work on this.  Looks like it's time for another rebase.
@helobinvn please see #1918 — would such an approach work for your needs?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

> @helobinvn please see #1918 — would such an approach work for your needs?

Yes, and plus 1 more option to only take the amount of the largest tags, then it would be exactly what my PR intended to do

> @helobinvn still working on this?

Hi @rhatdan, sorry I was not able to work on this recently, I will do it sometime next month. Thanks for understanding!
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
